headline: Why AAR (AIR) Shares Are Plunging Today
summary: Shares of aviation and defense services provider AAR CORP (NYSE:AIR) fell 7.3% in the afternoon session after reports revealed concerns that rising oil prices, driven by geopolitical conflict in the Middle East, would negatively impact the aviation industry.

headline: Delta names new CFO amid sweeping leadership changes
summary: Delta veteran Erik Snell will step in as CFO as the airline aims to navigate the impact from the Iran war, including a spike in fuel prices and canceled flights to Middle East hubs.

headline: Analyst cuts American to Neutral, favors Delta and United amid fuel cost pressures
summary: Investing.com -- In a note to clients on Thursday, analysts at Rothschild & Co Redburn downgraded American Airlines to Neutral while maintaining a negative stance on Southwest Airlines and reiterating more positive views on Delta Air Lines and United Airlines, citing rising jet fuel prices and growing capacity as key pressures on the U.S. airline sector.

headline: Delta Suspension Of Tel Aviv Flights Tests Margins And Growth Narrative
summary: Delta Air Lines (NYSE:DAL) has suspended its New York to Tel Aviv flights through at least March 22 due to the escalating Middle East conflict. The conflict has led to broad regional airspace disruptions, airport operational issues, and a rise in oil prices that is pressuring airline fuel costs. Delta is facing material operational and financial challenges as one of the most exposed U.S. carriers on the disrupted route. For context, NYSE:DAL last closed at $63.83, with the share price down...
